qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: SSRF via workflow import-from-url endpoint with no internal-IP validation
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes credential theft via name-based credential resolution and permission checker bypass in the credential pipeline. This finding describes SSRF in the workflow import-from-url endpoint. The vulnerable code locations, attack surface, and root cause are completely different. The advisory affects the credential resolution/execution path, while this finding affects the workflow import endpoint.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding matches the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory (CVE-2026-33663) describes a specific credential theft vulnerability in n8n involving name-based credential resolution and permission checker bypass that allows authenticated users to steal plaintext secrets from generic HTTP credentials. The ssrf-workflow-import-from-url finding describes SSRF in a different n8n endpoint (workflow import) with a different vulnerability class and root cause.
evidence: Advisory CVE-2026-33663 affects n8n credential pipeline: name-based resolution without ownership enforcement + permission checker bypass for generic HTTP credential types (httpBasicAuth, httpHeaderAuth, httpQueryAuth)

ssrf-workflow-import-from-url finding targets GET /rest/workflows/from-url endpoint with missing SSRF protection in workflows.controller.ts - different endpoint, different vulnerability class, different root cause
The finding does not reference the advisory's affected code paths: credential resolution logic, permission checking for generic HTTP credentials, or the specific authorization bypass mechanism
